title: Censor-aware next-stop time sidecar with `survival:aft`
summary: This target is naturally a time-to-event problem: each row has a remaining number of laps until the next pit, and final stints are right-censored.
rationale: This target is naturally a time-to-event problem: each row has a remaining number of laps until the next pit, and final stints are right-censored. A survival sidecar uses more label structure than a binary next-lap target and can learn a smoother imminence surface.
implementation_hint: For each driver-race trajectory, derive `laps_until_next_pit`; use exact intervals `[t,t]` when a future pit exists and right-censored `[t,+inf)` when the stint ends without another stop. Train XGBoost `survival:aft`, then convert predicted time-to-pit into monotone features such as `1/pred_time`, `pred_time<=1.5`, and race-normalized hazard rank for the main classifier.
expected_effect: Often helps near-boundary cases by separating ‘pit soon but not this lap’ from ‘not for many laps,’ which plain binary training compresses together.
risk: If CV is not strictly grouped by race/driver chronology, the derived time-to-event label can leak future structure; survival predictions may also be hard to calibrate directly to probabilities.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
